mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving near-term corporate earnings revisions that push S&P 500 prices higher over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE drops below 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside stable consumer prices signals benign supply-side inflation, allowing real output growth to translate directly into higher equity multiples and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO and CPIAUCSL divergence fails to predict positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: In periods of high credit availability, increases in housing starts amplify construction demand shocks, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days as building materials and financial sectors rally.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to generate positive S&P 500 returns when RSAFS exceeds 400000 in the walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect sustained employment expansion, which supports aggregate consumption and corporate revenue growth, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Month-over-month PAYEMS increases do not correlate with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns, with Rank IC remaining below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 2.5% annualized, the Federal Reserve faces binding inflation constraints that limit rate cuts and compress equity risk premiums, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 2.5% in more than 50% of triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between real GDP growth and employment growth indicates rising labor productivity without proportional wage inflation, boosting corporate profit margins and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 and PAYEMS divergence fails to predict positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: During supply chain stress indicated by low industrial production, rising unemployment signals demand destruction rather than structural adjustment, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over 5 days as recession fears mount.
failure_condition: Rising UNRATE alongside low INDPRO does not produce negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances.
